page: Microsoft Azure DC8eds_v5 is a virtual machine instance belonging to the DCEDV5 server family. It is built on the x86_64 CPU architecture and features 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel processor. The instance is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. Virtualization is managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

This instance includes 232 GB of local storage and is capable of utilizing Premium Storage. The local disk configuration provides direct-attached storage capacity for temporary files or caching. It does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses, and there are no onboard GPU accelerators.

From a resource density and cost-efficiency perspective, the DC8eds_v5 offers a balanced allocation of dedicated compute cores and memory. The inclusion of local storage and Premium Storage compatibility makes it suitable for workloads requiring fast disk access without the overhead of external storage arrays. This virtual machine is designed for memory-optimized workloads, database hosting, enterprise applications, and general-purpose business systems that require dedicated CPU resources and local disk caching.
description: The Microsoft Azure DC8eds_v5 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the DCEDV5 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated Intel vCPUs and 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance is equipped with 232 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage, making it suitable for applications requiring fast local disk access. It does not include any GPU accelerators or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Positioned as a memory-optimized and storage-capable instance, the DC8eds_v5 is designed for database hosting, enterprise applications, and general-purpose workloads that benefit from dedicated CPU allocation and local SSD caching.
